title: nymphaea odorata subsp. odorata
intro: Nymphaea odorata subsp. odorata, also known as the American white water lily, is a subspecies of aquatic plant belonging to the Nymphaea genus, characterized by its fragrant flowers and edible parts. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Nymphaea odorata subsp. odorata include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
